https://www.anthropic.com/claude-code.text: Claude Code is Anthropic's dedicated agentic coding tool. It runs in the terminal and IDE, drives multi-step software development tasks, and is narrower in scope than a general-purpose agent platform.

https://openai.com/index/codex.text: Codex is OpenAI's coding agent. As of 2026 it is being repositioned as a general-purpose agent platform, so it spans more than agentic coding. It runs in the cloud, desktop, and IDE.

https://openai.com/index/codex-cli.text: Codex CLI is the terminal client in the Codex family. It operates independently in the terminal and is part of OpenAI's Codex lineup.

https://antigravity.google.text: Antigravity 2.0 is Google's agent-first development platform, announced at Google I/O 2026. It ships as a desktop app plus an SDK and connects to AI Studio and Firebase.

https://antigravity.google/cli.text: Antigravity CLI is a Go rewrite that replaces Gemini CLI. It runs in the terminal and is the recommended migration target for Gemini CLI users.

https://jules.google.text: Jules is Google's asynchronous coding agent. It runs in the cloud and is also Google's analog for remote and async agent control.

https://developers.googleblog.com/gemini.text: Gemini CLI is being retired for individual and consumer tiers and replaced by Antigravity CLI. Enterprise Gemini Code Assist access is retained.

https://openai.com/codex.text: Codex Cloud runs asynchronous coding tasks that can be started and checked remotely. It is part of OpenAI's Codex platform and lets developers kick off and monitor runs without a local session.
